children.opsecNote: Passive threat database queries

children.name: mlsecproject / combine
children.description: Tool for gathering and normalizing threat intelligence feeds from public sources.
children.bestFor: Threat feed aggregation
children.output: Normalized IOC data in CSV or CRITs format
children.opsecNote: Local processing of public feeds

children.name: hostintel - keithjjones Github
children.description: Modular Python application to collect host and malicious IP intelligence.
children.bestFor: Host intelligence gathering
children.output: Intelligence data in CSV format
children.opsecNote: Local tool for passive host analysis

children.name: massive-octo-spice - csirtgadgets Github
children.description: CSIRT threat intelligence platform (deprecated - use bearded-avenger v3 instead).
children.bestFor: Legacy CSIRT intelligence platform
children.output: Aggregated threat data
children.opsecNote: Local platform for threat aggregation

children.name: Scam Database
children.description: User-contributed database of scam reports searchable by phone, email, and website.
children.bestFor: Scam and fraud reporting
children.output: Unverified scam reports and details
children.opsecNote: Passive lookup of community reports

children.name: Bot Scout
children.description: Service tracking bot signatures (names, IPs, emails) to prevent automated spam and abuse.
children.bestFor: Bot and spam detection
children.output: Bot activity and spam records
children.opsecNote: Passive honeypot-based detection

children.name: APTnotes
children.description: Repository of public documents, whitepapers, and articles about APT campaigns.
children.bestFor: APT research and threat analysis
children.output: Threat reports and analysis
children.opsecNote: Passive access to public threat research

children.name: HoneyDB
children.description: Honeypot network providing real-time IoT and server threat intelligence via REST API.
children.bestFor: IoT honeypot intelligence
children.output: Honeypot interaction logs and threat data
children.opsecNote: Passive honeypot-based intelligence

children.name: Pulsedive
children.description: Free threat intelligence platform for enriching IPs, URLs, domains, and IOCs from OSINT feeds.
children.bestFor: IOC enrichment and risk scoring
children.output: Enriched threat intelligence and risk factors
children.opsecNote: Passive querying of enrichment database

children.name: Mr.Looquer IOC Feed - 1st Dual Stack Threat Feed
children.description: Threat intelligence feed providing IOC data with IPv4/IPv6 dual stack support.
children.bestFor: IOC threat feed access
children.output: IOC threat intelligence feed
children.opsecNote: Passive threat feed consumption

children.name: REScure Cyber Threat Intelligence Project
children.description: Community-driven cyber threat intelligence project providing IOC feeds and research.
children.bestFor: Threat intelligence feeds
children.output: IOC threat intelligence
children.opsecNote: Passive threat feed access

children.name: Malware Exploit TTP Database
children.description: Malware exploit database documenting security vulnerabilities in malware families (offline).
children.bestFor: Malware exploit research
children.output: Exploit documentation and vulnerability details
children.opsecNote: Passive lookup when functional

children.name: Mitre TTPs
children.description: MITRE ATT&CK framework: globally-accessible knowledge base of adversary tactics and techniques.
children.bestFor: Threat modeling and TTP analysis
children.input: Search for tactics, techniques, or threat groups
children.output: Technique descriptions and mitigation strategies
children.opsecNote: Passive research of public threat intelligence

children.name: Fake Name Generator
children.description: Generates realistic synthetic identities including names, addresses, SSNs, and biographical details for persona creation during OSINT operations.
children.bestFor: Creating cover personas, sock puppet accounts, test data generation
children.output: Complete synthetic identity with name, address, DOB, contact info, and biographical details
children.opsecNote: No account required; generated personas have no real-world ties unless combined with other accounts.

children.name: Fake Identity Generator
children.description: Generates random fake identities including names, addresses, and personal details for use in anonymous account creation and persona testing.
children.bestFor: Persona creation, anonymous account registration, test data
children.output: Synthetic name, address, email, phone, and biographical data
children.opsecNote: Passive tool with no data submission; generated identities are synthetic with no real-person ties.

children.name: This Person Does Not Exist
children.description: Generates photorealistic AI-synthesized human faces using StyleGAN2; each page load produces a unique, non-existent person’s portrait.
children.bestFor: Creating realistic profile photos for sock puppet accounts or personas
children.input: No input required (random generation on each load)
children.output: High-resolution photorealistic portrait of a non-existent person
children.opsecNote: Purely generative; no data submitted and no real person involved.

children.name: Random User Generator
children.description: Open-source API that generates randomized user profiles including names, photos, addresses, and credentials for development and OSINT persona building.
children.bestFor: Bulk persona generation, test account data, application seeding
children.input: Nationality, gender, quantity, and field filters via API or web
children.output: JSON user objects with name, location, email, phone, photo, DOB, and login data
children.opsecNote: API-based generation with no data retention; all output is synthetic.

children.name: Faker.js
children.description: JavaScript library for generating massive amounts of fake data including names, addresses, companies, and more; browser demo now hosted at fakerjs.dev.
children.bestFor: Programmatic fake data generation for application testing and persona data
children.input: Data category selection (name, address, company, etc.)
children.output: Synthetic data strings for selected categories
children.opsecNote: CDN demo offline (rawgit.com shut down); active project continues at fakerjs.dev as a local npm package.

children.name: Tor Download (T)
children.description: Official download page for the Tor Browser and Tor daemon, which route traffic through an encrypted relay network to anonymize origin IP addresses.
children.bestFor: Anonymous web browsing, accessing .onion sites, circumventing censorship
children.input: Tor Browser installation on local system
children.output: Anonymized browsing sessions with exit node IP masking
children.opsecNote: Strong anonymization for browsing; does not protect against browser fingerprinting or JavaScript exploits.

children.name: Freenet Project (T)
children.description: Decentralized, censorship-resistant peer-to-peer network (rebranded Hyphanet) for anonymous file sharing and publishing without central servers.
children.bestFor: Anonymous file sharing, censorship-resistant publishing, distributed data storage
children.output: Access to Freenet network content and anonymous publishing capability
children.opsecNote: Fully decentralized with no central server; anonymization depends on network size and configuration.

children.name: I2P Anonymous Network (T)
children.description: Garlic-routing overlay network designed for anonymous internal communications and services, with support for email, torrents, and web browsing.
children.bestFor: Anonymous internal network communications, darknet service hosting, peer-to-peer privacy
children.output: Access to I2P eepsites and anonymous outproxy browsing
children.opsecNote: Strong anonymization via layered encryption; primarily designed for internal I2P network traffic rather than clearnet access.

children.name: VPN Comparisons - That One Privacy Site
children.description: Comprehensive VPN and email provider comparison charts evaluating privacy policies, jurisdiction, logging, and security features; original site no longer active.
children.bestFor: Comparing VPN providers for anonymity operations
children.output: Side-by-side VPN provider feature and policy comparisons
children.opsecNote: Site domain was sold and original content is no longer available. Use Privacy Guides (privacyguides.org) as replacement.

children.name: UserAgentString.com
children.description: Database of known user agent strings for browsers, bots, and devices; helps identify what browser/OS a user agent string represents or find strings to spoof.
children.bestFor: Identifying user agent strings, finding UA strings to use for spoofing
children.output: Browser type, OS, version, and device details extracted from UA string
children.opsecNote: Reference lookup only; useful for selecting believable user agents for browser spoofing during OSINT operations.

children.name: WhatIsMyBrowser.com
children.description: Detects and reports your current browser, operating system, and plugins as seen by websites; useful for verifying anonymization and user-agent spoofing effectiveness.
children.bestFor: Verifying browser fingerprint and user-agent visibility during OSINT sessions
children.input: No input required (detects current browser automatically)
children.output: Browser name, version, OS, plugins, and shareable detection link
children.opsecNote: Visiting this site sends your real browser data to their servers; use only to test anonymization setups.

children.name: User Agent String Decoder
children.description: Parses and decodes user agent strings into human-readable browser, OS, and device information components.
children.bestFor: Parsing raw UA strings from logs, understanding browser fingerprint components
children.output: Parsed browser family, version, OS, and device type breakdown
children.opsecNote: Submits the UA string to the tool’s server for parsing; does not expose your real browsing context if used with a test string.

children.name: IP / DNS Leak Detection
children.description: Comprehensive leak detection tool that tests for IP, DNS, WebRTC, and IPv6 leaks to verify VPN or Tor anonymization is working correctly.
children.bestFor: Verifying VPN effectiveness, detecting IP and DNS leaks before OSINT operations
children.input: No input required (automatically tests current connection)
children.output: Detected IP, DNS servers, WebRTC leaks, and geolocation data
children.opsecNote: Connecting to this service reveals your current IP and DNS to their servers; use only to test anonymization setup before operations.

children.name: JonDonym
children.description: Anonymization verification tool from the JonDonym project that checks IP, browser headers, cookies, and other identifiers for privacy leaks.
children.bestFor: Comprehensive anonymization verification, browser privacy audit
children.input: No input required (tests current connection automatically)
children.output: IP address, browser headers, cookie status, JavaScript fingerprint, and anonymity score
children.opsecNote: Reveals current network and browser data to their servers during the check; use only to verify anonymization.

children.name: DNS leak test
children.description: Tests whether DNS queries are leaking outside a VPN tunnel by checking which DNS servers resolve your lookups, potentially exposing your ISP.
children.bestFor: Verifying DNS query routing through VPN, detecting ISP DNS exposure
children.input: No input required (runs standard and extended DNS leak tests)
children.output: List of DNS servers handling your queries with ISP and location details
children.opsecNote: Test queries reveal which DNS servers handle your lookups; reveals real DNS servers to the testing service.

children.name: DNS Leak Tests
children.description: Alternative DNS leak testing service that identifies whether DNS requests bypass your VPN tunnel and expose your real ISP’s DNS servers.
children.bestFor: Secondary DNS leak verification, cross-checking VPN DNS configuration
children.output: DNS server IPs, provider names, and country locations for all resolving servers
children.opsecNote: Sends DNS probe queries that can be logged; use as part of pre-operation anonymization verification.

children.name: IPv6 Leak Tests
children.description: Tests whether IPv6 connectivity is leaking your real IP address outside a VPN tunnel that only routes IPv4 traffic.
children.bestFor: Detecting IPv6 leaks through IPv4-only VPNs, dual-stack network verification
children.output: IPv6 address if leaking, confirmation of leak status, and remediation guidance
children.opsecNote: May expose your real IPv6 address to the testing service; use before OSINT operations on dual-stack networks.

children.name: Email Leak Tests
children.description: Tests whether your email client or webmail service leaks your real IP address in email headers when sending messages.
children.bestFor: Verifying email IP privacy, testing anonymous email service configurations
children.input: Send a test email to a provided address
children.output: IP addresses found in email headers with assessment of anonymization effectiveness
children.opsecNote: Requires sending a test email that includes your real email headers; use a dedicated test account.

children.name: Perfect Privacy
children.description: IP and anonymization check tool from the Perfect Privacy VPN service that verifies visible IP, DNS servers, and WebRTC status.
children.bestFor: Pre-operation anonymization verification, IP and DNS leak check
children.output: Visible IP address, DNS servers, WebRTC status, and browser fingerprint summary
children.opsecNote: Sends your current IP and browser data to a third-party VPN provider’s servers; use only for anonymization testing.

children.name: WebRTC Leak Test
children.description: Tests whether WebRTC is exposing your real IP address even when using a VPN, as WebRTC can bypass VPN tunnels and reveal local and public IPs.
children.bestFor: Detecting WebRTC IP leaks in browsers, verifying WebRTC disable effectiveness
children.input: No input required (automatic browser WebRTC detection)
children.output: Local IP, public IP via WebRTC, and VPN IP with leak assessment
children.opsecNote: Uses WebRTC in your browser to detect leaks; revealing your real IPs to the testing service is expected during this test.

children.name: LetMeCheck.it
children.description: Multi-protocol anonymization test that checks IP, DNS, WebRTC, and browser fingerprinting for VPN and proxy users.
children.bestFor: Comprehensive pre-operation anonymization check covering multiple leak vectors
children.output: IP, DNS, WebRTC, and fingerprint analysis with overall anonymization rating
children.opsecNote: Exposes your current connection data to the testing service; use only for anonymization setup verification.

children.name: Trace My IP
children.description: IP address lookup and geolocation tool that reveals current IP, ISP, location, and additional network metadata visible to websites.
children.bestFor: Verifying visible IP and geolocation before OSINT operations
children.input: No input required (detects current IP automatically)
children.output: IP address, ISP, country, region, city, coordinates, and connection type
children.opsecNote: Logs your IP on visit; use only for verifying anonymization setup before conducting actual OSINT work.

children.name: Browser Statistics
children.description: W3Schools browser market share statistics showing usage distribution across browser types and versions to help craft believable user agent strings.
children.bestFor: Selecting realistic browser user agents, understanding browser fingerprint believability
children.output: Browser market share percentages by browser family and version
children.opsecNote: Reference page only; visiting W3Schools does not expose sensitive operational data.

children.name: WhatsMyBrowser.org
children.description: Identifies your current browser version and settings, providing a shareable link that displays your full browser fingerprint to others.
children.bestFor: Verifying browser fingerprint, checking what browser version appears to websites
children.output: Browser name, version, OS, screen resolution, and shareable detection URL
children.opsecNote: Collects and stores browser fingerprint data; use only to verify anonymization effectiveness, not during live operations.

children.name: What browser am I using.co
children.description: Simple browser detection page that identifies your current browser name, version, and operating system.
children.bestFor: Quick browser identification check during anonymization setup
children.output: Browser name, version, and OS string
children.opsecNote: Basic browser detection; reveals current browser info to the site.

children.name: What Browser?
children.description: Browser detection service that identifies your browser and offers guidance on updating to current versions; useful for fingerprint verification.
children.bestFor: Browser version verification, fingerprint audit during OSINT setup
children.output: Current browser name, version, and update status
children.opsecNote: Basic browser detection page; use to verify what browser identity is visible before OSINT operations.

children.name: IP2Proxy
children.description: Database and lookup service that detects whether an IP address belongs to a proxy, VPN, Tor exit node, or hosting provider to assess anonymization visibility.
children.bestFor: Checking if your VPN/proxy IP is flagged, verifying anonymization infrastructure quality
children.output: Proxy type classification (VPN/TOR/hosting/residential), ISP, and country
children.opsecNote: Passive IP lookup; useful for checking if your exit node IPs are flagged in commercial proxy databases before conducting operations.

children.name: NoScript (T)
children.description: Firefox/Chrome security extension that blocks JavaScript, Java, Flash, and other active content by default to prevent browser fingerprinting and code execution from untrusted sites.
children.bestFor: Browser hardening, preventing JavaScript-based fingerprinting and tracking during OSINT
children.output: Selective script blocking with per-site whitelist control
children.opsecNote: Reduces active fingerprinting surface significantly; some OSINT tools require JavaScript and may need temporary whitelisting.

children.name: Firefox-debloat
children.description: Collection of Firefox configuration tweaks and policies to disable telemetry, enhance privacy, and reduce browser fingerprinting surface.
children.bestFor: Hardening Firefox for OSINT operations, reducing browser telemetry and tracking
children.output: Hardened Firefox instance with reduced fingerprinting and telemetry
children.opsecNote: Local configuration changes only; no data transmitted. Reduces but does not eliminate browser fingerprinting.

children.name: Browser Leaks
children.description: Comprehensive browser privacy and security testing suite covering IP, DNS, WebRTC, canvas fingerprinting, font enumeration, and dozens of other browser data leakage vectors.
children.bestFor: Deep browser fingerprint analysis, identifying all data leak vectors before OSINT operations
children.output: Detailed reports on WebRTC, canvas, WebGL, fonts, plugins, and all other browser identifiers
children.opsecNote: Sends comprehensive fingerprint data to their servers; use to audit your anonymization setup but not during live operations.

children.name: Self-Destructing Cookies (T)
children.description: Firefox extension that automatically deletes cookies when a tab is closed to prevent persistent tracking; functionality now largely covered by Firefox’s built-in Enhanced Tracking Protection.
children.bestFor: Automatic cookie cleanup to prevent cross-session tracking
children.output: Automatic cookie deletion on tab close with per-site whitelist support
children.opsecNote: Reduces cookie-based tracking; modern Firefox’s built-in cookie management may provide equivalent protection.

children.name: BrowserSpy.dk
children.description: Danish browser information and fingerprinting test site that reveals detailed browser capabilities including plugins, fonts, screen properties, and other identifiers.
children.bestFor: Detailed browser fingerprint inventory, identifying what data is visible to websites
children.output: Comprehensive browser property list including plugins, fonts, screen data, and capabilities
children.opsecNote: Collects detailed browser fingerprint; use only for anonymization setup auditing.

children.name: LocaBrowser.com
children.description: Browser geolocation testing tool that checks what location data your browser exposes via the HTML5 Geolocation API to websites.
children.bestFor: Testing browser geolocation exposure, verifying location permission settings
children.input: No input required (requests browser geolocation permission)
children.output: Latitude, longitude, and accuracy of browser-reported geolocation
children.opsecNote: Requests and logs your browser’s geolocation; verify location masking is working before conducting location-sensitive OSINT.

children.name: Privacy Guides
children.description: Community-maintained guide recommending privacy-respecting software, services, and best practices; covers VPNs, browsers, operating systems, email, and more.
children.bestFor: Selecting privacy tools, building an OSINT operational security toolkit
children.output: Curated tool recommendations with evaluation criteria and comparison tables
children.opsecNote: Reference resource only; no data submission required.

children.name: Just Delete Me
children.description: Directory of direct links to account deletion pages for hundreds of online services, rated by deletion difficulty to help with digital footprint removal.
children.bestFor: Removing digital footprint, deleting OSINT research accounts, cleaning up persona accounts
children.output: Direct deletion link, difficulty rating (easy/medium/hard/impossible), and deletion instructions
children.opsecNote: Reference directory only; actual account deletion requires interacting with each service directly.

children.name: OptOut Credit Prescreen
children.description: Official US consumer opt-out service operated by major credit bureaus to remove yourself from pre-screened credit and insurance offer lists.
children.bestFor: Reducing data broker exposure, removing from credit prescreening lists
children.input: Personal information (SSN, DOB, address) for opt-out request
children.output: Confirmed opt-out from credit and insurance prescreening offers
children.opsecNote: Requires submitting real personal information to major credit bureaus; only use for actual personal privacy protection, not during OSINT operations.

children.name: Credit Freeze
children.description: Intel Techniques tutorial covering the complete process for freezing credit at all major and specialty bureaus to prevent identity theft and data broker access.
children.bestFor: Protecting personal identity, hardening against identity theft, reducing data broker profiles
children.output: Step-by-step credit freeze procedures for all major credit bureaus
children.opsecNote: Reference guide only; actual freeze requires interacting with each bureau. Recommended as part of comprehensive personal OPSEC.

children.name: Fake US Identities
children.description: Generates fake US identity data including names, addresses, SSNs, and driver’s license numbers for test and persona creation purposes.
children.bestFor: Generating synthetic US identity data for persona creation and testing
children.output: Synthetic US name, address, SSN, DL number, and biographical data
children.opsecNote: Generates synthetic data only; using fake SSNs for fraudulent purposes is illegal — intended for legitimate testing and OSINT persona research only.

children.name: Social Media Fingerprint
children.description: Browser-based tool that detects which social media platforms you are currently logged into by exploiting cross-origin image loading timing differences.
children.bestFor: Checking social media session exposure, verifying browser compartmentalization effectiveness
children.input: No input required (automatic detection via browser)
children.output: List of social media platforms where active sessions are detected
children.opsecNote: Detects your active social media sessions locally in-browser; use to verify that OSINT browser profiles are properly isolated from personal accounts.

children.name: Privacy Tools
children.description: Privacy-focused tool recommendation site covering encrypted messaging, VPNs, browsers, operating systems, and email providers; now largely redirects to Privacy Guides.
children.bestFor: Privacy tool discovery, OPSEC toolkit building
children.output: Curated lists of privacy-respecting tools by category with brief descriptions
children.opsecNote: Reference site only. Note: privacytools.io has gone through ownership changes; privacyguides.org is the community-maintained successor.

children.name: Panopticlick
children.description: EFF browser fingerprinting test (now Cover Your Tracks at coveryourtracks.eff.org) that measures how uniquely identifiable your browser is across the web.
children.bestFor: Measuring browser fingerprint uniqueness, testing tracker blocking effectiveness
children.input: No input required (automatic browser fingerprint analysis)
children.output: Fingerprint uniqueness score, tracker blocking assessment, and detailed fingerprint components
children.opsecNote: EFF’s canonical browser fingerprint test; visit via Tor/VPN to test anonymization. URL now redirects to coveryourtracks.eff.org.

children.name: Intel Techniques - Hiding from the Internet
children.description: Michael Bazzell’s OSINT workbook covering comprehensive personal data removal, digital footprint reduction, and operational security techniques.
children.bestFor: Personal OPSEC hardening, digital footprint removal, data broker opt-outs
children.output: Structured workbook with data removal checklists, opt-out links, and privacy hardening steps
children.opsecNote: Reference document only; no data submitted to download. Highly recommended as an OPSEC foundation guide.

children.name: The Many Hats Club - Privacy Resources
children.description: Curated community resource list from The Many Hats Club security community aggregating privacy tools, guides, and operational security resources.
children.bestFor: Discovering community-vetted privacy and OPSEC resources
children.output: Categorized links to privacy tools, guides, and communities
children.opsecNote: Reference resource; community activity has declined. Content may be outdated.

children.name: The Hitchhiker’s Guide to Online Anonymity
children.description: Comprehensive, community-maintained guide to online anonymity covering threat modeling, Tor, VMs, operational security, and plausible deniability techniques.
children.bestFor: Deep OPSEC education, building anonymized OSINT infrastructure, threat modeling
children.output: Detailed technical guide covering anonymization stack, tools, and practices
children.opsecNote: Reference document only. One of the most thorough freely available OPSEC guides; regularly updated.

children.name: Awesome Opt-Out Guide 2026
children.description: Community GitHub repository compiling data broker opt-out procedures, privacy request templates, and removal links for 2026.
children.bestFor: Data broker removal, comprehensive opt-out across multiple platforms and services
children.output: Categorized opt-out links, templates, and step-by-step removal instructions
children.opsecNote: Reference guide only; actual opt-out requires direct interaction with each service.

children.name: Anonymouth - Document Anonymization (T)
children.description: Java-based authorship anonymization tool that analyzes and modifies writing style features to reduce stylometric identification of document authors.
children.bestFor: Anonymizing written documents, reducing authorship attribution risk in sensitive reports
children.input: Text documents for stylometric analysis and rewriting guidance
children.output: Authorship feature analysis and suggestions for stylistic modification to evade attribution
children.opsecNote: Runs locally; no data leaves the machine. Project is unmaintained (last active ~2017) but functional for academic and research use.

children.name: MAT2 (T)
children.description: Metadata Anonymisation Toolkit v2 — command-line tool that strips metadata from documents, images, audio files, and archives before sharing to prevent inadvertent disclosure.
children.bestFor: Removing EXIF/metadata from files before publication, cleaning documents prior to evidence submission or sharing
children.input: Files (PDF, DOCX, JPEG, PNG, MP3, etc.)
children.output: Metadata-stripped clean copies of input files
children.opsecNote: Runs entirely locally; highly recommended before sharing any file collected during OSINT operations.

children.name: Forensic OSINT (T)
children.description: Chrome extension for full-page web capture with evidence preservation; timestamps and disclosure-ready exports for legal investigations.
children.bestFor: Evidence capture, legal documentation, case-level review
children.output: Full-page screenshots with metadata, timestamps, and case organization
children.opsecNote: Browser-based evidence tool; creates timestamped records suitable for disclosure.

children.name: Fiddler (T)
children.description: Web debugging proxy that monitors, inspects, and logs HTTPS/WebSocket traffic between a computer and the internet for analysis.
children.bestFor: Network traffic analysis, web debugging, protocol inspection
children.output: Captured requests/responses, traffic logs, header analysis
children.opsecNote: Network-level traffic capture; may require target awareness and can trigger security alarms.

children.name: Burp Suite (T)
children.description: Integrated web application security testing platform with proxy, scanner, and manual testing tools for vulnerability assessment.
children.bestFor: Web application security testing, vulnerability discovery, traffic analysis
children.output: Security scan results, vulnerability reports, request/response logs
children.opsecNote: Active security testing tool; generates traffic and may trigger security alarms on target systems.

children.name: Page2Images (T)
children.description: Website screenshot and full-page capture API that converts URLs to images for various devices including desktop, mobile, and tablet.
children.bestFor: Website documentation, responsive testing, visual archiving
children.output: Full-page screenshots (PNG/JPG), mobile previews, device-specific captures
children.opsecNote: Remote screenshot capture via third-party service; passive documentation with no direct interaction.

children.name: Archive.is
children.description: Web archiving service that creates instant permanent snapshots of webpages as they appear at capture time.
children.bestFor: Web archiving, evidence preservation, historical snapshots
children.output: Archived webpage snapshot with metadata and permanent access URL
children.opsecNote: Creates public archive links; snapshots may be indexed and are publicly accessible.

children.name: Web Page Saver
children.description: Browser extension for saving complete webpages as single HTML files for offline access and evidence preservation.
children.bestFor: Document preservation, offline access, evidence capture
children.output: Single HTML file (.html/.mhtml) with embedded resources
children.opsecNote: Passive local document capture; no network activity after the initial page save.

children.name: Snapper (T)
children.description: Open-source Linux-based tool for automated batch website screenshotting and file capture across multiple hosts.
children.bestFor: Batch website screenshots, post-reconnaissance documentation
children.output: PNG screenshots, batch capture output
children.opsecNote: Open-source passive screenshot capture tool; sends HTTP requests to targets during capture.

children.name: Full Page Screen Capture Chrome Extension (T)
children.description: Chrome extension for one-click full-page screenshot capture of entire scrollable page content.
children.bestFor: Full-page documentation, scrollable content capture, visual evidence
children.output: PNG/JPG/PDF screenshots with export options
children.opsecNote: Browser-based passive capture; no traffic interception or modification.

children.name: EZR OSINT Sidebar (T)
children.description: Chrome extension providing integrated OSINT tools in a browser sidebar; extracts and documents images, emails, IPs, and metadata from web content.
children.bestFor: OSINT data extraction, metadata analysis, investigation documentation
children.input: Web content, images, emails, IP addresses
children.output: Extracted metadata, EXIF data, SHA-256 hashes, reverse search links, screenshots
children.opsecNote: Aggregates public OSINT data via sidebar-based information extraction; no active probing.

children.name: FRAPS (T)
children.description: Video recording and screen capture utility for Windows with FPS benchmarking and lossless video capture; last updated 2013 but still functional.
children.bestFor: Video game documentation, screen recording, FPS benchmarking
children.output: Video files (up to 120fps), screenshots (BMP), benchmark statistics
children.opsecNote: Screen recording tool that captures visible screen content locally; no network activity.

children.name: ShareX (T)
children.description: Free and open-source screenshot and screen recording tool for Windows supporting 80+ upload destinations and extensive automation workflows.
children.bestFor: Screenshot documentation, screen recording, quick sharing
children.output: PNG/JPG/GIF screenshots, MP4/WebM videos with audio
children.opsecNote: Open-source screen capture tool; supports local storage and optional cloud uploads.

children.name: Greenshot (T)
children.description: Free and open-source screenshot program for Windows and macOS with annotation, highlighting, and multi-format export capabilities.
children.bestFor: Screenshot capture, annotation, web page documentation
children.output: PNG/JPG/GIF screenshots with annotations and highlight options
children.opsecNote: Open-source passive screenshot tool; captures local screen content only.

children.name: Google Street View - Hyperlapse
children.description: Tool for creating timelapse and hyperlapse videos from Google Street View images along selected routes for geographic visualization.
children.bestFor: Route documentation, geographic visualization, temporal narrative
children.output: Hyperlapse video animation showing virtual travel along route
children.opsecNote: Uses public Google Street View data; passive visualization with no active reconnaissance.

children.name: ZeeMaps
children.description: Interactive map creation and sharing tool with unlimited markers, 3-level access control, and support for crowdsourced data input.
children.bestFor: Location mapping, investigation visualization, crowdsourced data collection
children.input: Locations via search, spreadsheet, CSV, KML, or GeoRSS
children.output: Interactive maps with markers, routes, reports, and data aggregation
children.opsecNote: Cloud mapping service; creates shareable maps that may be public unless access is restricted.

children.name: Timeline JS3
children.description: Open-source JavaScript timeline tool by Knight Lab that creates interactive multimedia timelines from Google Sheets or JSON data.
children.bestFor: Event documentation, chronological narrative, historical timeline creation
children.output: Interactive web timeline with multimedia support (Twitter, Flickr, YouTube, maps, etc.)
children.opsecNote: Web-based timeline visualization; published timelines may be publicly accessible.

children.name: GeoGuesser
children.description: Geography game for geolocation OSINT training; users observe visual clues in Street View panoramas to guess locations worldwide.
children.bestFor: Geolocation skills, visual intelligence analysis, landmark identification
children.output: Accuracy score, location guess feedback, player rankings
children.opsecNote: No active reconnaissance; purely observational gameplay using public imagery.

children.name: Verif!cation Quiz Bot
children.description: Daily OSINT verification challenges posted on X (Twitter), using a community-driven quiz format for image geolocation and source verification.
children.bestFor: Community OSINT challenges, image verification techniques, collaborative research
children.input: Shared images and verification questions from quizmasters
children.output: Community discussion threads, solution walkthroughs, and learning outcomes
children.opsecNote: No active probing; public community engagement via social replies.

children.name: Forensic OSINT KB Guides
children.description: Knowledge base of digital forensics guides for evidence capture and court-admissible documentation, including web capture and metadata analysis.
children.bestFor: Digital evidence preservation, chain-of-custody documentation, court-ready OSINT reporting
children.input: Target URLs or digital media requiring forensic capture
children.output: Timestamped artifacts, metadata analysis guidance, preserved evidence workflows
children.opsecNote: Evidence collection focus; requires proper methodology for investigative and legal contexts.

children.name: Open Source Intelligence Techniques
children.description: Professional OSINT training and certification by IntelTechniques with extensive video modules, documentation, and practical investigative exercises.
children.bestFor: Professional OSINT certification, structured curriculum, advanced investigative techniques
children.input: Student participation in course modules, notes, and guided practical exercises
children.output: Course completion, certification-track readiness, and advanced OSINT methodology
children.opsecNote: Instructor-led educational platform focused on passive investigative methodology.

children.name: Plessas
children.description: Expert-led OSINT training courses by Plessas Experts Network, from fundamentals to intensive hands-on investigation programs.
children.bestFor: Professional investigative training, corporate intelligence, legal and compliance investigations
children.input: Structured coursework, practical OSINT exercises, and instructor interaction
children.output: Course completion outcomes, investigative skill development, and training credentials
children.opsecNote: Educational environment centered on passive research techniques.

children.name: The OSINTion
children.description: Affordable OSINT training courses by Joe Gray, including people OSINT, business investigations, and blockchain-focused instruction.
children.bestFor: Accessible OSINT courses, CTF-style learning, people and business intelligence workflows
children.input: Live or remote class participation, practical exercises, and case-study analysis
children.output: Completed coursework, practical investigative techniques, and reusable OSINT workflows
children.opsecNote: Training-focused environment without active network probing requirements.

children.name: Smart Questions
children.description: Foundational guide by Eric S. Raymond on asking effective technical questions in open-source and technical communities.
children.bestFor: Research methodology, effective questioning, and stronger information-seeking habits
children.input: Reader engagement with essay guidelines and practical examples
children.output: Improved question framing, clearer research requests, and better community responses
children.opsecNote: Pure methodology reference; no target interaction or probing.
